name: Covariant
description: Covariant raised $222M for AI-driven warehouse robotics before Amazon hired all founders and 25% of staff in a $400M reverse acqui-hire, leaving a zombie company.
story: Founded in 2017 by former OpenAI researchers, Covariant developed AI systems that could guide robotic arms to pick and sort items in warehouses — a notoriously difficult robotics challenge. The company raised approximately $222 million and partnered with major logistics companies. In August 2024, Amazon executed a reverse acqui-hire, paying over $380 million in licensing fees plus $20 million to hire all three co-founders and roughly 25% of the workforce. The deal was structured to avoid antitrust scrutiny. By 2025, a whistleblower complaint described Covariant as a 'zombie company' that existed only on paper to collect a final licensing payment from Amazon. No public updates have been issued since the deal.
review_flags: acqui-hired but no acquisition price found in story; total_loss set to funding_raised
